Luntian Meridian Holdings, Inc. (LMHI), a Philippines issuer, issues this Privacy and MNPI Handling Notice pursuant to RA 10173 (Data Privacy Act) and the Securities Regulation Code.
We collect personal data apparent or directly ascertainable to identify an individual, including employee names, company IDs, payroll bank details, and work emails, for HR, tax, banking, and capital-markets purposes.
For financing transactions, we also process trader onboarding files, custodial wallet tags, and pre-deal allocation notes that may constitute material non-public information under Philippine SEC guidance.
During the confidential “Project Balangay” ESG-linked token pilot and a contemplated sustainability-linked note, covered persons are under a blackout window until formal disclosure to the SEC and the local stock exchange.
An insider list will be maintained for Project Balangay and the transition note draft term sheet (indicative coupon 3.8% p.a.), and any tipping or selective disclosure is prohibited.
Information barriers segregate the digital assets desk from the sustainability reporting unit; access to MNPI is role-based and logged by the Compliance Office.
On 12 May 2026 we detected a credential-stuffing attempt on an LMHI vendor portal; while no exfiltration is confirmed, incident details are restricted until completion of forensics and regulatory notification.
Personal data may be transferred to processors (e.g., Baybay Audit Partners and Starholme Custody Services) under data sharing agreements with standard contractual clauses and NPC-compliant safeguards.
Retention: HR and tax records are retained for 10 years; deal files, insider lists, and cyber logs are retained for seven years or longer if an SEC inquiry is pending.
Data subjects may file a request for access, correction, or erasure by writing to Mr. Eusebio L. Cataran, Data Protection Officer, at dpo@lmhi.example.ph or +63 28 555 0199.
We verify DSARs via two-factor checks against internal directories and will respond within 15 working days, subject to legal holds relating to MNPI or ongoing investigations.
Commercial negotiations with Dalikmata Virtual Asset Exchange for custodial fees (draft range: 14–22 bps) are confidential and may be redacted from DSAR copies until publicly disclosed.
Marketing note: our public webinar on crypto custody and ESG reporting uses sample scenarios and does not reference live token offerings, insider lists, or blackout calendars.
We do not collect government ID images unless mandated by KYC laws, and any such copies are stored encrypted in-country with access limited to Compliance and Treasury Operations.
Questions on this Notice may also be raised with the National Privacy Commission or the Philippine SEC; however, LMHI remains the primary contact and controller for processing described herein.
